overview:
## Discussion

It is impossible to distinguish between a lock event caused by an explicit request and one caused by a keychain that locked itself because of a timeout. Therefore, the `pid` parameter in the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecKeychainCallbackInfo] structure does not contain useful information for this event. Note that when the login session terminates, all keychains become effectively locked; however, no `kSecLockEvent` events are generated in this case.

# SecKeychainEvent.lockEvent

**Case**

Indicates a keychain was locked.

## Declaration

```swift
case lockEvent
```

## Discussion

It is impossible to distinguish between a lock event caused by an explicit request and one caused by a keychain that locked itself because of a timeout. Therefore, the `pid` parameter in the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecKeychainCallbackInfo] structure does not contain useful information for this event. Note that when the login session terminates, all keychains become effectively locked; however, no `kSecLockEvent` events are generated in this case.
title: SecKeychainEvent.lockEvent
